justification: All pass criteria met: only v2 content used (AC1 + AC3, no AC2 from v1); domain rule uses absolute prohibition language ('cannot be disabled under any circumstances') citing legal requirement; AC3 present with exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED; contradictions_resolved section explicitly notes v2 supersedes v1 and explains why; MJ's 403 enforcement is correctly reflected in architecture_guidance with server-side guard.

observations.text: Clean separation — AC2 from v1 is completely absent from acceptance_criteria, only the three v2-aligned criteria appear
contradictions_resolved section provides excellent traceability: explains the v1 internal tension (AC2 vs domain rule) and how v2 resolved it
Architecture guidance correctly specifies that transactional_emails_enabled is not a writable field and the 403 guard is server-side and caller-independent
Low-confidence areas and assumptions are well-calibrated — flags genuine ambiguities (default value, true no-op case) without fabricating answers
task_context immediately orients Shaq: v2 supersedes v1, legal review drove the change, no contradictions remain
